notes: foreground-subagent for mistral-vibe: the parent uses the builtin Task tool to spawn ONE synchronous explore subagent that reads a seeded README.md and reports its heading, then the parent replies 'done'. Spec asserts the arc: parent ready→working at spawn, a child session materializes under <parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl (task.py save_dir=session_dir/'agents'; session_logger.py persists messages.jsonl+meta.json), the child is parent_linked to the parent, the parent stays working across the child's lifetime (the Task tool-call awaits the child), and parent working→ready once the child ends. The parent↔child link is now LIVE (commit 3d89d5e9): deriveVibeParentSessionID reads the parent off the .../<parent>/agents/<child>/messages.jsonl path — matching the id sessionIDFromPath registers the parent under — and the existing subagent machinery emits parent_linked, holds the parent working, and tracks SubagentCount. record ports the driver's wait_turn seam (driver=gap:wait_turn) before driving. Single-birth adapter (session id = the transcript directory, sessionIDFromPath), birth anchored to start unpinned. Observations: model mistral-medium-3.5 (live config.active_model), tokens_nonzero (sidecar stats.context_tokens → turn_done), cost_nonzero=false (parser never reads stats.session_cost; mistral-vibe absent from core/pkg/capacity).

text: Parent session_<...> born in ready when its transcript directory appears (single-birth; id from the transcript directory)
User Task-tool prompt drives the parent ready → working (spec bullet 1)
Subagent transcript materializes under <parent>/agents/<explore_...>/messages.jsonl — fswatcher discovers a new SessionState for the child

Detector emits parent_linked binding child.session_id → parent.session_id (spec bullet 2: ParentSessionID = parent.session_id). deriveVibeParentSessionID resolves the parent off the child's .../agents/<child>/ path

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: Parent stays working while the child is alive — the Task tool-call awaits the child loop (spec bullet 4)
After the child reports back and the parent replies 'done' (turn_done), the parent goes working → ready (spec bullet 5)
